examples: We do not need to proselytise either by our speech or by our writing.
That is because the church sends so many of its young overseas to proselytise, where they meet other cultures.
Mr Lebrecht's admiration for the composer is a wonderful thing, but he is trying too hard to proselytise: other people may feel the same way about Bach or Mozart or Wagner.
Members of the Eastern Orthodox church have been offended by the attempts of the Catholic church to proselytise in Russia.
